evals.name: Lowpass filter for noisy audio
evals.description: Design a lowpass FIR filter to clean up a noisy audio signal. Tests the full workflow: spec gathering, architecture selection, designfilt, verification with freqz.
evals.prompt: I have audio sampled at 44.1 kHz that has high-frequency noise above 4 kHz. Design a lowpass filter to clean it up. I want linear-phase for offline batch processing. Passband ripple should be under 0.1 dB and stopband attenuation at least 60 dB.

assertions.description: Generated filter design code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses designfilt for filter design
Specifies SampleRate in designfilt call
Verifies filter response with freqz
Does not use deprecated subplot for multi-panel figures
Computes transition bandwidth metrics (trans_bw or trans_pct) before selecting architecture
assertions.expected: Computes trans_bw = Fstop - Fpass or trans_pct = 100 * trans_bw / Fs, or discusses transition bandwidth

assertions.description: States passband ripple and stopband attenuation measurements against spec
assertions.expected: Reports measured ripple and attenuation values and compares to the 0.1 dB / 60 dB spec

assertions.description: Writes code to .m file instead of inline evaluation
assertions.expected: Uses write to file then run_matlab_file, not multi-line inline evaluate_matlab_code

evals.name: Narrow transition band bandpass filter
evals.description: Design a bandpass filter with a very narrow transition band, requiring efficient alternatives to single-stage FIR. Tests the architecture selection decision tree and efficient-filtering knowledge.
evals.prompt: I need a bandpass filter for a 48 kHz signal. Passband is 100-120 Hz, stopband edges at 80 Hz and 140 Hz. Need at least 60 dB stopband attenuation. This is for offline analysis so zero-phase is fine. What are my options and which is most efficient?

assertions.description: Generated filter design code runs without error in MATLAB
Identifies this as a narrow transition band problem
assertions.expected: Computes trans_pct and identifies it as less than 2%, or explicitly states the transition band is narrow relative to sample rate

assertions.description: Discusses multirate or IFIR as an efficient alternative
assertions.expected: multirate OR IFIR OR multistage OR decimat

assertions.description: Compares at least two candidate architectures with cost metrics
assertions.expected: Compares MPIS, filter order, or computational cost for at least two approaches (e.g., single-stage FIR vs multirate, or FIR vs IFIR)

assertions.description: Uses designfilt or fdesign for filter design
Uses filtfilt for zero-phase filtering since user specified offline
assertions.expected: Uses filtfilt() or explicitly states filtfilt for zero-phase offline filtering

assertions.description: Does not use deprecated subplot

evals.name: Streaming IIR filter design
evals.description: Design a streaming-compatible IIR filter. Tests IIR-specific knowledge: SOS form, stability, System objects, and the streaming vs offline distinction.
evals.prompt: Design a real-time streaming lowpass filter for sensor data at 1000 Hz sample rate. Cut off above 50 Hz. I need minimum latency and low computational cost -- IIR is preferred. The filter will process data frame-by-frame in a loop.

assertions.description: Generated filter design code runs without error in MATLAB
Designs an IIR filter (Butterworth, Chebyshev, or Elliptic)
assertions.expected: butter OR cheby OR ellip OR IIR

assertions.description: Uses SOS form for IIR filter to avoid numerical issues
assertions.expected: Uses SecondOrderSections, SOS, sos, or SystemObject=true in designfilt, or explicitly converts to SOS

assertions.description: Does not recommend filtfilt for streaming (filtfilt is offline-only)
Shows frame-by-frame processing pattern with state preservation
assertions.expected: Shows a loop that processes frames, using a System object or filter state (zi/zf) to maintain continuity between frames

assertions.description: Uses designfilt with SampleRate
Verifies filter response (magnitude and/or group delay)
assertions.expected: Uses freqz, grpdelay, or filterAnalyzer to verify the design

evals.name: Compare filter designs in Filter Analyzer
evals.description: Design multiple candidate filters and compare them visually in Filter Analyzer. Tests filterAnalyzer usage, multi-filter comparison workflow, and analysis option configuration.
evals.prompt: I have vibration data sampled at 10 kHz and need to isolate a 200 Hz component. I'm not sure whether to use an FIR or IIR bandpass filter. Can you design both, compare them side by side in Filter Analyzer showing magnitude and group delay, and recommend which one to use? Passband is 180-220 Hz, stopband edges at 150 Hz and 250 Hz, at least 50 dB stopband attenuation. This is for offline analysis.

assertions.description: Generated filter design and comparison code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses filterAnalyzer to compare designs visually
Designs at least two candidate filters (FIR and IIR) for comparison
assertions.expected: Creates at least two digitalFilter objects or filter designs, one FIR and one IIR

assertions.description: Uses designfilt with SampleRate for filter design
Shows both magnitude and group delay displays in Filter Analyzer
assertions.expected: Configures or mentions both magnitude and group delay views in Filter Analyzer

assertions.description: Makes a recommendation with quantitative justification (order, group delay, ripple)
assertions.expected: Recommends FIR or IIR with measured numbers (filter order, group delay, passband ripple, or stopband attenuation)

assertions.description: Does not use subplot for multi-panel figures

evals.name: Notch filter for power line removal
evals.description: Design a notch filter to remove 60 Hz power line interference. Regression test for notchiir requiring FilterOrder parameter.
evals.prompt: I have a biomedical signal sampled at 1000 Hz with 60 Hz power line interference. Design a notch filter to remove the 60 Hz component. This is for offline analysis.

assertions.description: Generated notch filter code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses designfilt with notchiir response type
Includes FilterOrder parameter in designfilt call
Specifies SampleRate
Verifies notch depth at 60 Hz
assertions.expected: Uses freqz or filterAnalyzer to check attenuation at or near 60 Hz

evals.name: Streaming bandpass IIR with scalar edges
evals.description: Design a streaming IIR bandpass filter. Tests bandpass IIR scalar edge gotcha, SystemObject=true, and streaming constraints.
evals.prompt: I need a streaming bandpass filter for an audio application. Pass 300 Hz to 3400 Hz, sample rate is 16 kHz. I don't care about phase.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses designfilt bandpassiir with SystemObject=true
Uses scalar edge parameters (not vector form) for IIR bandpass
assertions.expected: Uses PassbandFrequency1, PassbandFrequency2, StopbandFrequency1, StopbandFrequency2 — NOT PassbandFrequency=[f1 f2]

assertions.description: Does not use filtfilt for streaming
Specifies SampleRate

evals.name: Notch plus lowpass combo
evals.description: Design a notch at 60 Hz plus a lowpass FIR for linear-phase. Tests multi-filter pipeline and notch+lowpass combination.
evals.prompt: Remove 60 Hz power line interference from a biomedical signal sampled at 500 Hz. I also need to lowpass at 150 Hz. Offline processing, linear phase required.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses designfilt notchiir for the notch filter
Includes FilterOrder in the notch designfilt call
Uses FIR for the lowpass to guarantee linear phase
assertions.expected: Uses designfilt lowpassfir — not lowpassiir, since user requires linear phase

assertions.description: Specifies SampleRate in designfilt calls

evals.name: Extreme multirate lowpass
evals.description: Design a lowpass at 0.5 Hz from a 50 kHz signal. Tests extreme narrow transition requiring multirate approach.
evals.prompt: Design a lowpass filter to extract the DC component up to 0.5 Hz from a vibration sensor signal sampled at 50 kHz. Offline, don't care about phase.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Recognizes extreme narrow transition and does NOT use single-stage FIR
assertions.expected: Computes trans_pct << 2% and uses multirate or multistage approach, not single-stage FIR

assertions.description: Uses multirate or IFIR approach
assertions.expected: resample OR designMultirateFIR OR decimat OR ifir OR multistage

assertions.description: Specifies SampleRate in designfilt call for the core filter

evals.name: Streaming highpass narrow transition
evals.description: Design a streaming linear-phase highpass with narrow transition. Tests streaming + linear-phase + narrow transition interaction.
evals.prompt: I need a streaming highpass filter at 20 Hz to remove DC offset and low-frequency drift from an accelerometer at 4 kHz. Linear phase is required. Stopband at 10 Hz with 80 dB attenuation.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses FIR-based design for linear phase (not IIR)
assertions.expected: Uses FIR filter — not IIR, since user requires linear phase in streaming

assertions.description: Recognizes narrow transition and considers efficient alternatives
assertions.expected: Computes trans_pct = 0.25% and discusses multirate or IFIR, or acknowledges high tap count

assertions.description: Does not use filtfilt for streaming
Specifies SampleRate

evals.name: Zero-phase filtfilt doubling awareness
evals.description: Design a zero-phase filter with filtfilt. Tests whether the agent accounts for filtfilt doubling the effective attenuation.
evals.prompt: Design a zero-phase lowpass filter for offline analysis of an EMG signal sampled at 2 kHz. Passband up to 450 Hz, stopband at 500 Hz, with 80 dB stopband attenuation.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses filtfilt for zero-phase
Acknowledges filtfilt doubles effective attenuation
assertions.expected: States that filtfilt squares the magnitude response or doubles attenuation in dB

assertions.description: Does not use SystemObject with filtfilt
Specifies SampleRate

evals.name: IFIR constant rate narrow transition
evals.description: Design with IFIR when internal rate change is prohibited. Tests IFIR knowledge path.
evals.prompt: I have a 48 kHz sampled audio signal and need to lowpass at 200 Hz with stopband at 250 Hz and 60 dB rejection. I cannot change the internal sample rate. Offline, linear phase.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Recognizes narrow transition and considers IFIR
assertions.expected: Computes trans_pct ~0.10% and uses ifir() or mentions IFIR as an approach since rate change is prohibited

assertions.description: Excludes multirate since user prohibits rate change
assertions.expected: Explicitly states multirate is excluded because user cannot change internal sample rate

assertions.description: Uses IFIR or acknowledges constant-rate constraint
assertions.expected: ifir OR IFIR OR constant-rate OR constant rate

assertions.description: Specifies SampleRate

evals.name: Bandstop zero-phase
evals.description: Design a bandstop filter applied with filtfilt. Tests bandstop design and IIR scalar edge gotcha.
evals.prompt: Design a bandstop filter to remove a 400-600 Hz interference band from a 8 kHz sampled signal. Offline, zero-phase required.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses designfilt for bandstop design
Uses filtfilt for zero-phase
If IIR, uses scalar edge parameters (not vector form)
assertions.expected: If bandstopiir, uses StopbandFrequency1/StopbandFrequency2 — not vector form

assertions.description: Specifies SampleRate

evals.name: MPIS cost comparison three architectures
evals.description: Compare cost of FIR, IIR, and multirate for a narrow transition. Tests cost() usage and architecture comparison.
evals.prompt: Compare the cost of filtering a 96 kHz signal with a lowpass at 1 kHz (stopband 1.2 kHz, 60 dB rejection). Show me the MPIS for single-stage FIR, single-stage IIR, and a multirate pipeline. Streaming, don't care about phase.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Designs all three architectures and reports MPIS via cost()
assertions.expected: Uses cost().MultiplicationsPerInputSample on System objects for FIR, IIR, and multirate pipeline

assertions.description: Multirate MPIS is lower than single-stage FIR
assertions.expected: Multirate pipeline cost is significantly less than single-stage FIR cost

assertions.description: Uses filterAnalyzer to compare candidates
Specifies SampleRate

evals.name: High-order IIR SOS stability
evals.description: Design a high-order IIR filter. Tests SOS form requirement for numerical stability.
evals.prompt: Design a highpass Butterworth IIR filter of order 14 at 200 Hz for a 10 kHz signal. Streaming, don't care about phase.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses designfilt for the design
Uses SOS form, not [b,a] polynomial for order 14 IIR
assertions.expected: Uses designfilt (internal SOS) or SystemObject=true, NOT [b,a] = butter(14,...) which is numerically unstable

assertions.description: Warns about numerical stability for high-order IIR
assertions.expected: Notes or warns that order 14 IIR requires SOS form for stability

assertions.description: Does not use filtfilt for streaming

evals.name: Bandstop IIR minimum-order elliptic
evals.description: Design a minimum-order bandstop IIR using elliptic method. Regression test for bandstopiir requiring PassbandRipple1/2 (not singular PassbandRipple) and singular StopbandAttenuation.
evals.prompt: Design a bandstop IIR filter to notch out a 200-400 Hz band from a 4 kHz signal. Use elliptic design for minimum order. I need at least 60 dB stopband attenuation and no more than 1 dB passband ripple. Offline, zero-phase.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB on first attempt
Uses designfilt with bandstopiir response
Uses numbered PassbandRipple1 and PassbandRipple2 (not singular PassbandRipple) for minimum-order bandstopiir
assertions.expected: PassbandRipple1 and PassbandRipple2 both appear in the designfilt call

assertions.description: Uses singular StopbandAttenuation (not StopbandAttenuation1/2) for bandstopiir
Specifies SampleRate

evals.name: filtfilt halved-spec design recipe
evals.description: Design a zero-phase filter using the filtfilt halving recipe. Regression test for exact doubling: design at Rs/2, Rp/2, then verify effective response.
evals.prompt: Design a zero-phase lowpass filter for offline processing of an EEG signal at 500 Hz. I need effective stopband attenuation of at least 80 dB at 55 Hz (stopband edge) and effective passband ripple under 0.5 dB up to 45 Hz. Use filtfilt for zero-phase. Show me the design accounting for filtfilt's effect on the specs.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses filtfilt for zero-phase filtering
Designs the filter at halved specs (Rs/2=40 dB, Rp/2=0.25 dB) to account for filtfilt doubling
assertions.expected: Designs with StopbandAttenuation=40 (or close to 40) and PassbandRipple=0.25 (or close to 0.25), not the full 80/0.5 spec

assertions.description: Verifies the effective (post-filtfilt) response, not just the single-pass response
assertions.expected: Computes or mentions effective/squared magnitude response (2*20*log10(abs(H)) or |H|^2) and checks against the original 80 dB / 0.5 dB specs

assertions.description: Specifies SampleRate

evals.name: Filter Analyzer mixed sample rates
evals.description: Compare two filters designed at different sample rates in Filter Analyzer. Tests FilterNames, SampleRates parameters, and multi-Fs overlay.
evals.prompt: I have a two-stage audio pipeline: first an anti-alias lowpass at 48 kHz (passband 3.4 kHz, stopband 4 kHz, 80 dB rejection), then after decimating by 6 a cleanup lowpass at 8 kHz (passband 3 kHz, stopband 3.4 kHz, 60 dB rejection). Design both filters and compare them side by side in Filter Analyzer with proper labels and sample rates. Show magnitude and group delay. Offline, linear phase.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses filterAnalyzer for comparison
Passes FilterNames to label the two filters
assertions.expected: FilterNames parameter in filterAnalyzer call with descriptive labels for both filters

assertions.description: Passes SampleRates to handle different Fs for each filter
assertions.expected: SampleRates parameter in filterAnalyzer call with [48000 8000] or equivalent two-element specification

assertions.description: Specifies SampleRate in designfilt calls

evals.name: Visualize and compare filters without naming tool
evals.description: Design two filters and ask to visualize and compare them without naming filterAnalyzer. Tests whether the skill steers toward filterAnalyzer vs fvtool or manual plots.
evals.prompt: Design an FIR and an IIR lowpass filter for a 20 kHz signal, both cutting off at 2 kHz with 60 dB rejection. I want to see their frequency responses and group delays overlaid so I can pick the better one. Offline, don't care about phase.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses filterAnalyzer for visual comparison (not fvtool, fdatool, or manual freqz subplots)
assertions.expected: filterAnalyzer() called — NOT fvtool, fdatool, filterDesigner, or manual freqz/grpdelay plots

assertions.description: Does not use deprecated fvtool
Uses designfilt with SampleRate
Makes a quantitative recommendation between FIR and IIR
assertions.expected: Recommends one design over the other citing measured numbers (order, group delay, ripple, or attenuation)
